evidence: Profiles define explicit minimal, standard, and full steps, gates, artifacts, and verification policies.
Minimal route skips planning artifacts but still requires adoption, scope, targeted verification, review, and state-save; high-risk hints route to full.
artifactDebt reports required, missing, and tracked-outside-route artifacts without changing gate pass/fail decisions.
Validator fails if minimal route steps expand or production readiness is added to minimal defaults.
Documentation describes profiles and artifactDebt; no runtime trust boundary introduced.
Workspace policy documents smallest-fitting-profile rule; no external input handling introduced.
